**13 files total** — 3 pages, 2 components, 1 service, CSS per component/page, plus `main.jsx` and `index.css`.

---

## 2. `web/src/App.jsx`

```1:34:web/src/App.jsx
import { BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, Navigate } from 'react-router-dom';
import Layout from './components/Layout';
import Login from './pages/Login';
import Dashboard from './pages/Dashboard';
import Evaluate from './pages/Evaluate';

function ProtectedRoute({ children }) {
  const token = localStorage.getItem('token');
  if (!token) return <Navigate to="/login" replace />;
  return children;
}

function App() {
  return (
    <BrowserRouter>
      <Routes>
        <Route path="/login" element={<Login />} />
        <Route
          path="/"
          element={
            <ProtectedRoute>
              <Layout />
            </ProtectedRoute>
          }
        >
          <Route index element={<Dashboard />} />
          <Route path="evaluate/:bookletId" element={<Evaluate />} />
        </Route>
      </Routes>
    </BrowserRouter>
  );
}

export default App;
```

**Key points:**
- React Router v6 with a `ProtectedRoute` guard checking `localStorage` for a JWT token.
- Two protected routes: `/` → `Dashboard`, `/evaluate/:bookletId` → `Evaluate`.
- `Layout` wraps all protected routes (likely renders `Header` + `<Outlet />`).

---

## 3. `web/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`

```1:136:web/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { api } from '../services/api';
// ...
```

**Key points:**
- On mount calls `api.eval.dashboardSummary()` and `api.eval.pendingBooklets()`.
- Renders four `SummaryCard` components: **Answer Sheets**, **Evaluated**, **Pending**, **Rejected** — values from the API response (`totalAnswerSheets`, `evaluated`, `pending`, `rejected`).
- Renders a `<table>` of pending booklets with columns: Document Number, UG/PG, Branch, Year, Semester, Subject, Marks, Status.
- Each row's "Document Number" is an `<a href="/evaluate/{BookletID}">` link.
- `StatusBadge` maps `Evaluated | Rejected | Allocated | InProgress` to CSS classes.
- Right sidebar has placeholder `Calendar`, `Events`, and `Today's Activities` panels.

---

## 4. `web/src/pages/Evaluate.jsx`

```1:86:web/src/pages/Evaluate.jsx
import { useParams } from 'react-router-dom';
// ...
```

**Key points:**
- **Currently a static UI placeholder** — reads `:bookletId` from the URL but all data (name, course, subject, marks) is hardcoded.
- Layout: top info bar → three-panel main area (thumbnail strip | document viewer | marks sheet).
- Document viewer shows a placeholder div with a comment: *"Connect to actual page images from `Eval_Booklets.FilePath`"*.
- Marks table has hardcoded rows with `<input type="number">` for allotted marks.
- Bottom annotation toolbar with ✓, ✕, ○, ?, —, 💬 buttons.
- No API calls are wired up yet in this component.

---

## 5. `web/src/services/api.js`

```1:73:web/src/services/api.js
const API_BASE = '/api';
// ...
```

**Key points:**
- Central `request()` helper wraps `fetch`, attaches `Authorization: Bearer <token>` from `localStorage`, and auto-redirects to `/login` on 401.
- Throws on `data.success === false` — assumes the API envelope `{ success, data, message }`.
- Exports `api` object with three namespaces:

| Namespace | Methods |
|-----------|---------|
| `api.auth` | `login(username, password)`, `profile()` |
| `api.eval` | `dashboardSummary()`, `pendingBooklets(limit, offset)`, `openBooklet(bookletId)`, `startEvaluation(bookletId, type)`, `saveMarks(evaluationId, details)`, `submitEvaluation(evaluationId, totalMarks, totalPages)`, `logPageVisit(evaluationId, pageNumber)` |
| `api.files` | `pageUrl(bookletId, pageNumber)` — returns a URL string, not a fetch |

---

## 6. `web/src/components/Header.jsx`

```1:22:web/src/components/Header.jsx
import './Header.css';
// ...
```

**Key points:**
- Static component — no props or state.
- Left: `TU` logo block + "Technical University" brand text.
- Centre nav: "Home" and "View" links (both pointing to `/`).
- Right: two icon badges with hardcoded counts (13, 23), hardcoded username "Ravi Rajan", and an avatar div.
- No logout functionality or dynamic user info from token yet.

---

## 7. `api/src/modules/` — Full File Tree

```
api/src/modules/
├── auth/
│   ├── auth.routes.js
│   ├── auth.controller.js
│   ├── auth.service.js
│   └── auth.repository.js
├── eval/
│   ├── eval.routes.js
│   ├── eval.controller.js
│   ├── eval.service.js
│   └── eval.repository.js
└── scan/
    ├── scan.routes.js
    ├── scan.controller.js
    ├── scan.service.js
    ├── scan.repository.js
    └── files.routes.js
```

**13 files** — 3 modules (auth, eval, scan), each with routes/controller/service/repository. `scan` also has a separate `files.routes.js`.

---

## 8. `api/src/modules/auth/auth.routes.js`

**Key points:**
- Instantiates `AuthRepository(getEvalDb())` → `AuthService` → `AuthController` at module load time.
- Two routes:
  - `POST /api/auth/login` — `authLimiter` (rate limit) + `auditLog` + `validateBody` (username/password required strings) → `controller.login`
  - `GET /api/auth/profile` — `auditLog` + `authenticate` middleware → `controller.profile`
- Full OpenAPI JSDoc annotations present.

---

## 9. `api/src/modules/auth/auth.controller.js`

**Key points:**
- `login`: destructures `{ username, password, source }` from body, delegates to `service.login()`, returns `ok(res, result, 'Login successful')`.
- `profile`: reads `req.user.userId` (set by JWT middleware), delegates to `service.getProfile()`.
- Uses class field arrow functions (`login = async (req, res, next) => {…}`) so `this` is auto-bound.

---

## 10. `api/src/modules/auth/auth.service.js`

**Key points:**
- `login(username, password, source)`:
  - `source === 'scan'` → dynamically imports `getScanDb()` and calls `repo.findScanUserByUsername()` (ScanningDB lookup).
  - `source === 'eval'` (default) → calls `repo.findUserByUsername()` (EvaluationDB lookup).
  - Checks `IsActive`, then `bcrypt.compare()` against `PasswordHash`.
  - Signs JWT with `{ userId, username, fullName, roleId, roleName, roleLevel, locationId, source }` payload, `env.jwt.secret`, `env.jwt.expiresIn` (default 8h).
- `getProfile(userId)`: fetches by ID, returns sanitized user object (no hash).

---

## 11. `api/src/modules/eval/eval.routes.js`

**Key points:**
- All routes globally guarded with `authenticate`, `auditLog('eval')`, and `evalLimiter`.
- Seven routes:

| Method | Path | Controller Method | Purpose |
|--------|------|-------------------|---------|
| GET | `/dashboard/summary` | `dashboardSummary` | Stats cards |
| GET | `/booklets/pending` | `pendingBooklets` | Pending table (limit/offset query params) |
| GET | `/booklet/:bookletId` | `openBooklet` | Load booklet for evaluation |
| POST | `/evaluation` | `startEvaluation` | Create evaluation session |
| PUT | `/evaluation/:evaluationId/marks` | `saveMarks` | Auto-save marks (upsert) |
| POST | `/evaluation/:evaluationId/submit` | `submitEvaluation` | Finalize + variance check |
| POST | `/evaluation/:evaluationId/page-visit` | `logPageVisit` | Compliance audit trail |

---

## 12. `api/src/modules/eval/eval.controller.js`

**Key points:**
- Thin controller — all business logic delegated to `EvalService`.
- `pendingBooklets`: parses `limit`/`offset` from query, passes with `req.user.userId`.
- `startEvaluation`: passes `bookletId`, `req.user.userId`, `type` (Primary/Secondary), and `req.user.username`.
- `saveMarks`: parses `evaluationId` as integer, passes `details` array.
- `submitEvaluation`: passes `totalMarks` and `totalPages` for page-visit completeness check.
- `logPageVisit`: only passes `pageNumber` (other fields like duration/zoom are in the body but not destructured here — the service presumably reads `req.body` or the controller is minimal).

---

## 13. `api/src/config/env.js`

```1:40:api/src/config/env.js
import { config } from 'dotenv';
config();

const env = {
  nodeEnv: process.env.NODE_ENV || 'development',
  port: parseInt(process.env.PORT || '4000', 10),
  clientUrl: process.env.CLIENT_URL || 'http://localhost:5173',
  swaggerUiOrigin: process.env.SWAGGER_UI_ORIGIN || 'http://localhost:5173',

  scanDb: {
    host: process.env.SCAN_DB_HOST || 'localhost',
    port: parseInt(process.env.SCAN_DB_PORT || '3307', 10),
    user: process.env.SCAN_DB_USER || 'root',
    password: process.env.SCAN_DB_PASSWORD || '',
    database: process.env.SCAN_DB_NAME || 'ScanningDB',
  },

  evalDb: {
    host: process.env.EVAL_DB_HOST || 'localhost',
    port: parseInt(process.env.EVAL_DB_PORT || '3307', 10),
    user: process.env.EVAL_DB_USER || 'root',
    password: process.env.EVAL_DB_PASSWORD || '',
    database: process.env.EVAL_DB_NAME || 'EvaluationDB',
  },

  jwt: {
    secret: process.env.JWT_SECRET || 'dev-secret-change-me',
    expiresIn: process.env.JWT_EXPIRES_IN || '8h',
  },

  storage: {
    mode: process.env.STORAGE_MODE || 'local',
    scanOutputPath: process.env.SCAN_OUTPUT_PATH || 'D:/ScanOutput',
  },

  logLevel: process.env.LOG_LEVEL || 'info',
};

export default env;
```

**Key points:**
- Two separate DB configs: `scanDb` (ScanningDB, default port 3307) and `evalDb` (EvaluationDB, same host/port).
- JWT secret defaults to `'dev-secret-change-me'` — must be overridden in production via `JWT_SECRET` env var.
- Storage mode (`local` vs presumably `s3`/`network`) controls how scanned files are served.
- Scan output path defaults to `D:/ScanOutput` — local Windows path for development.
- API server runs on port `4000` by default; web client expected on `5173` (Vite default).

---

## 14. `docker/mysql-init/01_schema.sql` — Table Definitions (Lines 1–200)

Creates two databases then defines the following tables in **ScanningDB**:

| Table | Primary Key | Key Columns |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `Scan_Locations` | `LocationID` (AI) | `LocationCode`, `LocationName`, `Address`, `IsActive` |
| `Scan_Workstations` | `WorkstationID` (AI) | `LocationID` (FK), `WorkstationCode`, `WorkstationName`, `IsActive` |
| `Scan_Roles` | `RoleID` (AI) | `RoleName`, `RoleHierarchyLevel` |
| `Scan_Users` | `UserID` (AI) | `Username` (unique), `PasswordHash`, `FullName`, `RoleID` (FK), `LocationID` (FK), `IsActive` |
| `Scan_Exams` | `ExamID` (AI) | `ExamCode`, `ExamName`, `ExamYear`, `IsActive` |
| `Scan_Papers` | `PaperID` (AI) | `ExamID` (FK), `PaperCode`, `PaperName`, `TotalPages`, `BookletPageCounts` |
| `Scan_Booklets` | `BookletID` (VARCHAR 100, PK) | `ExamID`, `PaperID`, `LocationID`, `WorkstationID`, `TotalPagesExpected`, `TotalPagesScanned`, `ValidationStatus`, `FileHashSHA256`, `FilePath`, `UploadStatus`, `IsExportedToEvaluation`, `ExportedAt`, `ScanDate` |
| `Scan_BookletPages` | `PageID` (BIGINT AI) | `BookletID` (FK), `PageNumber`, `ImagePath`, `PageHash` |

Every table has the full **common audit column set**: `CreatedBy`, `CreatedAt`, `CreatedFromIP`, `CreatedFromSystem`, `ModifiedBy`, `ModifiedAt`, `ModifiedFromIP`, `ModifiedFromSystem`, `IsDeleted`, `DeletedBy`, `DeletedAt`, `DeletedFromIP`. All use `ENGINE=InnoDB`, MySQL 8+, `utf8mb4_unicode_ci`.

`Scan_Booklets.ValidationStatus` values: `Valid`, `PageCountMismatch`, `BarcodeError`, `Duplicate`. The schema continues past line 200 into EvaluationDB tables (not shown above).
